steps.title: Recognize the vertex form
steps.content: The function $f(x) = 5\left(\frac{1}{4} - x\right)^2 + \frac{11}{4}$ is written in vertex form. The key insight is that the squared term $\left(\frac{1}{4} - x\right)^2$ equals zero when the expression inside equals zero, which occurs at $x = \frac{1}{4}$.

steps.title: Substitute and simplify
steps.content:
Substitute $x = \frac{1}{4}$ into the function:
$$f\left(\frac{1}{4}\right) = 5\left(\frac{1}{4} - \frac{1}{4}\right)^2 + \frac{11}{4}$$
The squared term becomes zero:
$$= 5(0)^2 + \frac{11}{4}$$
$$= \frac{11}{4}$$
